      • toStringMoney(x: Money): string
  • Exports

This module provides a Money type and related operations for handling monetary values in Nim. It includes support for multiple currencies, formatting, conversion rates, and basic arithmetic operations. The Money type is designed to be immutable by default, with mutable operations available through specific procs.

Imports

codes

Types

CurrencyRates = ref object
The CurrencyRates type holds conversion rates for a specific currency to other currencies. 
Money = object
  currency*: Currency        ## The currency of the money, represented as a `Currency` tuple containing
  units*: BigInt             ## The amount of money in the smallest unit (e.g., cents for USD, pennies for GBP).
  negative*, rtl*: bool ## `negative` indicates if the amount is negative, while `rtl` can be used
                        ## for right-to-left currency formatting if needed.
  rates*: CurrencyRates      ## A reference to `CurrencyRates` for handling conversion rates, if applicable.
The Money type represents a monetary value with a specific currency. 
MoneyConversionRates = ref object
  data*: CritBitTree[CurrencyRates] ## A crit-bit tree mapping base currency alpha3 codes to
                                    ## their corresponding `CurrencyRates` objects.
The MoneyConversionRates type is a singleton that holds conversion rates 
MoneyError = object of CatchableError
A general exception type for errors related to money operations. 
MoneyFormattingError = object of CatchableError
An exception type for errors that occur during money formatting.

Consts

moneyDefaultCurrency {.intdefine.}: int = 49
moneyDefaultUnit {.strdefine.}: string = "coins"

Procs

proc `!=`[M: Money](x, y: M): bool
proc `$`(symbol: Alpha3): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the symbol name 
proc `$`[M: Money](m: M | ref M): string
Return string representation of Money 
proc `$$`(x: int): Money {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Creates Money from x int 
proc `$$`(x: string; currency: Alpha3 = Alpha3(moneyDefaultCurrency)): Money {.
    inline, ...raises: [MoneyFormattingError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
An alias of formatMoney using currency set as moneyDefaultCurrency 
proc `%`(perc: float; x: Money): Money {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Applies perc to x Money 
proc `%`(perc: float; x: var Money) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Applies perc cut to x Money 
proc `*`[M: Money](x: M; y: BigInt): M
Multiplies x by y 
proc `*`[M: Money](x: M; y: float): M
Multiplies x by y Multiplies x by y with fixed-point precision 
proc `*`[M: Money](x: M; y: M): M
proc `*=`[M: Money](x: var M; y: int)
Multiplies x with y, and assing the result 
proc `*=`[M: Money](x: var M; y: M)
Multiplies x with y and assing the result 
proc `+`[M: Money](x, y: M): M
Addition of x and y Money. Returns the total as Money 
proc `+=`[M: Money](x: var M; y: M)
Addition of mutable x and y Money. Returns the total as Money 
proc `-`[M: Money](x, y: M): M
Subtract of x based on y. Returns the total as new Money 
proc `-=`[M: Money](x: var M; y: M)
Performs subtraction on the two operands and assigns the result to the mutable x. 
proc `.`[A: int; C: Alpha3](unit: A; currency: C): Money
proc `/`[M: Money](x: M; y: M): M
proc `/=`[M: Money](x: var M; y: int)
proc `/=`[M: Money](x: var M; y: M)
proc `<`[M: Money](x, y: M): bool
proc `<=`[M: Money](x, y: M): bool
proc `==`[M: Money](x, y: M): bool
proc `>`[M: Money](x, y: M): bool
proc `>=`[M: Money](x, y: M): bool
proc abs[M: Money](x: M): M
Returns the absolute value of x Money 
proc add(cvrates: MoneyConversionRates; alpha: Alpha3; rates: JsonNode) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Adds conversion rates for alpha currency to exchange 
proc add[M: Money](x: var M; y: varargs[M])
proc allocate(x: var Money; ratios: openArray[SomeNumber]): seq[Money]
Applies successive ratios to x Money (each ratio uses remaining balance) 
proc allocate[M: Money](x: var M; targets: int): seq[M]
An alias of div proc that allocates x Money to N targets 
proc amount(units: int; currency: Alpha3): Money {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create a new amount of Money 
proc avg[M: Money](x: varargs[M]): M
Returns the average 
proc coins(rep: uint = 1): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc contains(x: Money; currency: Alpha3): bool {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Determine if currency type of x Money is currency 
proc convert(x: Money; y: Alpha3; cvrates: MoneyConversionRates): Money {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, MoneyFormattingError, ValueError, Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Takes x Money and converts to y Alpha3 currency 
proc coupon(amount: var Money; discount: float) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Applies discount to amount 
proc coupon[M: Money](amount: var M; discount: M)
Applies discount to amount 
proc `div`(x: var Money; y: varargs[int]) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
A mutable proc to divide Money by y int 
proc `div`[M: Money](x: M; y: int): M
proc `div`[M: Money](x: var M; y: varargs[M])
A mutable proc to divide Money by y Money 
proc fmt(x: string; currency: Alpha3 = Alpha3(moneyDefaultCurrency)): Money {.
    inline, ...raises: [MoneyFormattingError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
An alias of `formatMoney` with `currency`
set as `moneyDefaultCurrency `
proc formatMoney(amount: string; currency: Alpha3): Money {.
    ...raises: [MoneyFormattingError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Format amount string to Money 
proc getCurrency(amount: Money): Currency {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hundreds(rep: uint = 1): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc inc(x: var Money; y: int = 1) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc incCent(x: var Money; y: int = 1) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Increment x Money by y cents 
proc incNotes(x: var Money; y: int = 1) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Increment x Money by y banknotes 
proc isCent(x: Money): bool {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Checks if x Money in cents 
proc isCurrency(x: Money; currency: Alpha3): bool {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
An alias of contains 
proc isNegative(x: Money): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Checks if x Money is negative 
proc isNotes(x: Money): bool {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Checks if x in banknotes 
proc isZero(x: Money): bool {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Checks if x Money is 0.00 
proc max[M: Money](x: varargs[M]): M
Returns the largest of the given Money objects 
proc min[M: Money](x: varargs[M]): M
Returns the smallest of the given Money objects 
proc multi[M: Money](x: var M; y: varargs[int])
A mutable proc to multiply Money with y int 
proc newMoney(x: BigInt; currency: Currency = Currencies[moneyDefaultCurrency]): Money {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Creates Money from x BigInt 
proc newMoney(x: float; currency: Currency = Currencies[moneyDefaultCurrency]): Money {.
    inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Creates Money from x float 
proc newMoney(x: int; currency: Currency = Currencies[moneyDefaultCurrency]): Money {.
    inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Creates Money from x int 
proc newMoney(x: string = "0"; currency: Alpha3 = Alpha3(moneyDefaultCurrency)): Money {.
    ...raises: [MoneyFormattingError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
An alias of formatMoney that creates a Money object with amount 0 
proc sub[M: Money](x: var M; y: varargs[M])
proc thousands(rep: uint = 1): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc toMoney(x: float64; currency: Alpha3 = Alpha3(moneyDefaultCurrency)): Money {.
    ...raises: [MoneyError, MoneyFormattingError, ValueError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Convert x float to Money 
proc toSmallestCurrency(x: Money): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Converts x Money to the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents) For example, EUR 5.00 would be converted to "500". 
proc toStringMoney(x: Money): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
An alias of $ proc for Money

Exports

Currencies, moneyUnitCoins, MoneyUnit, Currency, moneyUnitNotes, Alpha3
